name: Abell 2029
description: Abell 2029 is a galaxy cluster one billion light-years away in the constellation Virgo. Its central galaxy, which is IC 1101, is almost as large as it is. It emits 1 trillion times as much light as the Sun does. (About 10^34 light bulbs)

name: Albireo
description: Albireo is made up of three stars total. Here we are just showing the largest one. Two of the stars are close, and form a yellow dot. The third is blue and further away. The blue star and yellow stars are so far apart it takes them 100,000 years to complete one orbit.

name: Aldebaran
description: Aldebaran is not to be confused with Alderaan, which is a planet in Star Wars. Alderaan is very similar to Earth in size, day length, and water cover. However, Aldebaran is 4,900 times larger, rotates every 520 Earth days, and has no water.

name: Alnitak
description: Alnitak illuminates the Flame Nebula. Alnitak is also the easternmost star of the three stars that form Orion's belt. The other two are Alnilam and Mintaka, and all three appear very bright from planet Earth.

name: Alpha Centauri A
description: Alpha Centauri A is the largest star of the Alpha Centauri star system (composed of three stars), which is the closest star system to us. It is also the fourth brightest star in the night sky.

name: Alpha Centauri B
description: Alpha Centauri B is the second largest star in the Alpha Centauri star system. Its diameter is approximately 1.25 million kilometers, so it is slightly smaller than the Sun. Alpha Centauri A & B are so close they appear as a single star to us Earthlings.

name: Alpha Helix
description: The alpha helix is one of the secondary structures of proteins. Many proteins have alpha helices within them. Hemoglobin and myoglobin are proteins that are made of about 70% alpha helices!

name: Altair
description: Altair is one of the three stars in the Summer Triangle. The Summer Triangle is an acute triangle, which means that all the angles are less than 90°! The other two are Vega and Deneb. Like many other stars, Altair spins very quickly, bulging at the center!

name: AM Radio Wavelength
description: A and M are the first and thirteenth letters of the alphabet. One is a perfect square, while thirteen is not. We all have our differences, and we have to learn to accept them. That's the only way we'll live in harmony.

name: Amoeba
description: Amoebae are unicellular, which means there is only one cell. Some amoebae can grow to an incredible 700 micrometers, which is almost an entire millimeter.

name: Andromeda Galaxy
description: The Andromeda Galaxy is the Milky Way's twin. It's the nearest galaxy that is a similar size and shape. In a few billion years, the two galaxies will collide and form one larger elliptical galaxy - Milkomeda.

name: Angel Falls
description: Angel Falls is the tallest waterfall in the world. It is Venezuela's top tourist attraction. Most of the water evaporates into mist before hitting the ground. That's too bad. Or is it good? It's good.

name: Angstrom
description: An angstrom, which is named after Swedish physicist Anders Jonas Ångström, is one tenth of a nanometer. The symbol for an angstrom is Å. The angstrom is used to express the size of atoms or transistors.

name: Ant
description: You probably don't like ants. That's too bad. 15-25% of all land animals are ants! Whoa! That's a lot of ants! For every person on Earth, there are over one million ants!

name: Ant Nebula
description: The Ant Nebula, which is also called Mz 3, is expanding 180,000 kilometers per hour. It resembles an ant, so much so that it is named after an ant. For more about ants, zoom in 18 orders of magnitude.

name: Antares
description: Antares is a red supergiant very near the ecliptic, so it gets occulted (blocked) by the Moon quite often. Also, Antares weighs septillions of tons and is losing weight through solar wind, but this method is slow. The fast way is to explode, which it'll do later.

name: Apollo Lunar Module
description: This thing weighs 15,000 kilograms! That's a lot! It also landed on the Moon. Its landing inspired the arcade game "Lunar Lander” which is not easy. When I play, I always run out of fuel!

name: Archaeopteryx
description: Often considered the "first bird", Archaeopteryx lived during the Late Jurassic (about 150 million years ago). This small, feathered dinosaur, about the size of a modern raven, measured around 1.5 feet (0.5 meters) in length and had a mix of avian and dinosaurian traits. It possessed teeth, clawed wings, and a bony tail like a dinosaur but had feathers and possibly the ability for limited powered flight or gliding.

name: Arcturus
description: Arcturus is the third brightest star in the night sky, after Sirius and Canopus. It's the brightest star in the constellation of Boötes. Find it by following the handle of the Big Dipper.

name: Argentinosaurus
description: One of the largest known sauropods, Argentinosaurus lived during the Late Cretaceous (around 97–93 million years ago) in what is now South America. It could reach lengths of 98–115 feet (30–35 meters) and weigh as much as 100 tons. Argentinosaurus was a herbivore, with a long neck for reaching high vegetation and a massive, barrel-shaped body.

name: Asia
description: Asia is the Earth's largest continent, holding over four billion people – over half of the world's population. Africa is the second most populous continent, but only has one billion people.

name: Average Laptop Screen
description: Although different monitors have different pixel sizes, things on the screen are approximately the same size.

name: Average U.S. House
description: These houses of the United States have been getting larger. In the last 40 years, the average house has almost doubled in area! They are so large that you could fit 1,000 people in them! It would be pretty cramped, though.

name: Bacteriophage
description: The bacteriophage, or just "phage" for short, is a type of bacteria-infecting virus. I think they look like robots, not life forms. But it also could be argued that viruses are not alive at all.

name: Barnard's Loop
description: Barnard's Loop is a huge nebula and is only 1,600 light-years away. It covers 10 degrees of the sky (which is a lot). It spans over most of the constellation Orion! However, it's so faint you can only see it on dark nights, or with long exposure times. So sad.

name: Basketball
description: This basketball is probably a size 7 basketball, because a size 6 basketball is only 23 centimeters in diameter. This basketball, which is very round, is often pushed through a hoop.

name: Beach ball
description: Beach balls were invented in 1938, which was over 80 years ago. Beach balls are also colorful. In addition, they are very fun! The only beach ball that isn't fun is the beach ball of death.

name: Betelgeuse
description: Though stars can live for trillions of years, Betelgeuse is so big that it will only live for just over 8 million years. Since it is that old, it could explode at any moment! When it explodes, the supernova will be visible during the day. More likely however, it'll explode thousands of years from now.

name: Black Pillar
description: This little thingy is a part of the much larger Eagle Nebula. Even though it appears small in comparison to the Eagle Nebula, it's still larger than the solar system, by 10 - 20,000 times, depending on your definition of our Solar System.

name: Blinking Nebula
description: The Blinking Nebula is a planetary nebula that doesn't actually blink. It just seems to appear and disappear from view. There are two red FLIERs (Fast Low-Ionization Emission Regions) on opposite side of the nebula.

name: Blue Whale
description: The blue whale is the largest animal there is. Its weight, which is 180 metric tons, is greater than any other animal's.

name: Boeing 747
description: The Boeing 747, which flies, is an airplane. Since 1969, it's been shorter than a redwood tree. Before then, it didn't exist, so it wasn't shorter or longer. And yes, its wingspan is longer than the Wright brother's first flight (37 meters).

name: Boeing Everett Factory
description: The Boeing Everett Factory, with 13 million cubic meters of usable space, is one of the largest buildings in the world. The Great Wall of China and the Three Gorges Dam do not qualify as buildings.

name: Boomerang Nebula
description: The Boomerang Nebula is very cold, only one degree above absolute zero (-272° C)! That is colder than background radiation. Did you know the Boomerang Nebula is the coldest known object in the universe?

name: Bottom Quark
description: The bottom quark is also called the beauty quark, because it is so flawlessly beautiful. It is third generation and quickly decays into up and charm quarks.

name: Brunei
description: Brunei is a country in Southeast Asia. It is surrounded completely by Malaysia and the South China Sea. Roughly 450,000 people live in this country. They drive on the left side of the road.

name: Bubble Nebula
description: I love blowing bubbles. Blub-blub, pop! This nebula is not a bubble; it just looks like one! It was created by hot stellar wind. Oh, and it's an emission nebula, not a planetary nebula.

name: Buckyball
description: Buckyballs, which are also called buckminsterfullerenes, are made of 60 carbon atoms. The shape it forms is a truncated icosahedron, which is also the shape of a soccer ball! A solution made completely out of buckyballs is Violet.

name: Burj Khalifa
description: Burj Khalifa, which used to be called the Burj Dubai, is the tallest building in the world. No other building is taller than 828,000 millimeters tall. There's a swimming pool on the 76th floor.

name: California
description: California is nicknamed "The Golden State". In the United States, it is ranked first in population, third in land area, and eleventh in name length. California is home to roughly 40 million people, which is less than one percent of the world's population.

name: Callisto
description: Callisto is the most distant of the Galilean moons. It orbits outside all the other Galilean moons and has the most meteor impact craters of them all.

name: Canes Venatici Dwarf Galaxy I
description: The Canes Venatici Dwarf Galaxy I is 685,000 light-years away from us. If you zoom out to that scale, this galaxy will be a couple pixels across.

name: Canis Major Dwarf Galaxy
description: The Canis Major Dwarf Galaxy is the nearest galaxy, if you don't count the Milky Way itself. It lies between the arms of the Milky Way and is being pulled apart by the gravity of the Milky Way.

name: Capella
description: Capella is actually a quaternary star- that means four stars orbit in the system! Two stars are G-type giant stars, while the other two are M-type red dwarfs. The largest one is the one you see to the left. The Sun is also a type-G star, but it's a main-sequence one.

name: Carbon Atom
description: Carbon is the basis of all life on Earth. This is because carbon atoms can create four covalent bonds, which means it can bond in thousands of ways with oxygen, nitrogen, hydrogen, carbon, and other elements.

name: Carbon Nanotube
description: Carbon nanotubes are the strongest material yet discovered. As another bonus, they're lightweight. As a result, nanotubes have a lot of potential applications, including buildings, batteries, solar panels, and possibly even a space elevator.

name: Cartwheel Galaxy
description: Astronomers think the Cartwheel Galaxy had a collision with two nearby galaxies 200 million years ago. Actually, 600 million years ago, because the Cartwheel Galaxy is 400 million light-years away, so what we see of it is already 400 million years old.

name: Cat's Eye Nebula
description: The Cat's Eye Nebula is one of the most complex nebulae known. Did you know that the Cat's Eye Nebula is 8,000° C? That's pretty hot! The outer halo is even hotter, at 15,000° C.

name: Cave Nebula
description: The Cave Nebula is a very diffuse nebula. Of course, most nebulae as large as this one are faint, so you need long exposure times to see them. The Cave Nebula is also called Sh2-155 or Caldwell 9.

name: Cell Nucleus
description: Nuclei are pretty cool, and they can be found within all eukaryotic cells. (Human cells are eukaryotic.) The nucleus is where all the DNA is. During mitosis, the DNA will condense into chromosomes, and then the cell will split in two.

name: Ceres
description: Ceres is not a very good place for humans to live. Ceres is also the largest asteroid, and began the debate over the definition of "planet". For a time, Ceres was considered a planet, but then it wasn't.

name: Cesium Atom
description: Atom size is measured by the distance of the nuclei in covalent bonds. The shells don't actually look like this. Cesium is the largest atom, but its atomic number is not the highest. Why? See Helium Atom.

name: Charm Quark
description: The charm quark and the strange quark are the second generation of matter. They will quickly decay into up and down quarks, which are the first generation of matter. The charm quark is charming.

name: Charon
description: Charon is Pluto's only large moon. They are very close in size. They are so close, some people used to consider Pluto and Charon to be a double planet. Did you know the same side of Charon always faces Pluto, and the same side of Pluto always faces Charon?

name: Chicken Egg
description: The chicken egg is where baby chicks usually hatch. Many humans love to eat chicken eggs. Besides appearance, there is no difference between white chicken eggs and brown chicken eggs.

name: Chlorine Nucleus
description: A chlorine nucleus has 17 protons and anywhere from 11 to 34 neutrons. However, the majority of these isotopes will decay within minutes, if not seconds! The only stable isotopoes are chlorine-35 (18 neutrons) and chlorine-37 (20 neutrons). Even numbers of neutrons (especially magic numbers 2, 8, 20, 28, 50, 82, and 126) tend to be more stable than odd ones.

name: Chloroplast
description: Plants use chloroplasts to turn the Sun's energy into chemical energy, which is stored in glucose molecules! Chloroplasts are also what give plants their green color, because they have fabulous chlorophyll inside their thylakoid membranes.

name: Clay Particle
description: Because clay particles are so small, they become tightly packed, and water particles have trouble flowing through it. On the other hand, sand is much more coarse. Water just slips right through sand. Easy-peasy.

name: Coffee Bean
description: The coffee bean is about one centimeter across - and it's actually a fruit that resembles a bean. It's brown and has stripes, and sometimes, when you stare at it, you just forget that its diameter is 10^-29 of the known universe.

name: Common Earthworm
description: Don't you just love wriggly, slimy earthworms? Despite the urban legends, most species of earthworms will die if you cut them in half. Sometimes, the clitellum will survive and grow a new tail.

name: Compsognathus longipes
description: Compsognathus longipes, often called the "chicken of the Jurassic", was a nimble, turkey-sized dinosaur known for its slender frame and agile build. Zipping through ancient landscapes some 150 million years ago, this predator likely hunted small prey like insects and lizards, aided by its sharp teeth and swift legs. With a delicate, bird-like structure and keen senses, it was a glimpse into evolution's path toward modern avian life.

name: Cone Nebula
description: The Cone Nebula was discovered in 1785, on the day after Christmas. Did you know that the Cone Nebula is a dark nebula and a diffuse nebula? You can find it between Betelgeuse and Procyon.

name: Crab Nebula
description: The Crab Nebula is one of the most famous nebulae. It was also the first Messier object cataloged (M1)! The Crab Nebula's supernova was seen in 1054 AD and was so bright it could be seen during the day. It isn't even 1,000 years old!

name: Cruithne
description: Cruithne is known as Earth's second moon. It actually isn't Earth's moon. It just follows an elliptical orbit around the Sun that is near Earth's orbit, and its year is 364 days, close to Earth's year.

name: Deimos
description: Deimos was the smallest known moon in the Solar System for almost 100 years. You could walk around it in a day! Of course, you'd have to wear a space suit to survive in the vacuum of space. I don't think you could walk as fast in a space suit, though.

name: Deneb
description: Deneb is the brightest star in the constellation of Cygnus. Deneb is 2,600 light-years away, which makes it the most distant of the 25 brightest stars. If you lived on Mars, Deneb would appear above the North Pole - not Polaris.

name: Depth of the Mariana Trench
description: The Mariana Trench is a pretty deep trench. It is in the Pacific Ocean. Did you know that this lovely trench has been proposed as a site for dumping nuclear waste? That's a deep topic!

name: Distance Earth Has Travelled (Relative to Sun)
description: Earth has travelled almost half a million light-years relative to the Sun since its formation. (That's 4.5 billion years/orbits.) Earth is 10,000 times slower than light.

name: Distance from Comet Hale-Bopp to Sun (farthest)
description: The comet Hale-Bopp gets so far from the Sun, the tug of the planets changes its furthest point each visit. Arriving this time took 4,200 years, and it will take another 2,400 years to make another orbit.

name: Distance from Earth to Moon
description: The Earth and the Moon here are not to scale. If the distance were a meter, Earth would be the size of a quarter, and the moon would be a pea.

name: Distance from Earth to Sun
description: During the day, the Sun doesn't seem so far away. It's actually about 150 million kilometers away. That distance is an astronomical unit, which is called an AU for short.

name: Distance from Neptune to Sun
description: Neptune is the most distant known planet in the solar system. The distance from Neptune to the Sun is greater than the diameter of any known star!

name: Distance from Proxima Centauri to Alpha Centauri A
description: Proxima Centauri is very far away from Alpha Centauri A & B.

name: Distance from Sedna to Sun (farthest)
description: When Sedna is the farthest from the Sun, it is very far away. This point is called the "aphelion"! It takes Sedna over 10,000 Earth years to complete one orbit.

name: Distance from the Sun to Proxima Centauri
description: Proxima Centauri is the closest star to the Sun. However, it is still quite far away. 30 million Suns could fit between Proxima Centauri and the Sun in a straight line.

name: Distance from Voyager 1 to Earth
description: Over the last 45 years, Voyager 1 has traveled to a distance of 23 billion kilometers, which is the furthest any man-made object has gone, and every day it gets 1.5 million kilometers further away! You can't see it, even if you stare really, really hard!

name: Distance to the Andromeda Galaxy
description: At two million light-years away, the Andromeda Galaxy is the most distant object visible to the naked eye. However, without binoculars or a telescope, it will appear as a dim blur.

name: Distance to the Great Attractor
description: The Great Attractor is pulling thousands of galaxies, including the Milky Way, towards itself. Its mass is tens of thousands of times greater than the Milky Way's.

name: Distance to the Shapley Supercluster
description: The Shapley Supercluster is a supercluster of galaxies. It is one of the largest objects that collapses inward; anything larger expands with the universe.

name: DNA
description: DNA, which is a nucleic acid, stores the genetic information of almost all living things on Earth (except for some viruses.) A single human cell contains 3 meters of deoxyribonucleic acid, which holds about 25,000 genes in about 3 billion base pairs.

name: Dodo Bird
description: The dodo bird went extinct sometime during the 1600s. The name comes from the sound they make (or should I say used to make since they're now extinct). So yeah, they're gone.

name: Down Quark
description: There are six flavors of quarks. They are up, down, strange, charm, top, and bottom. The smaller a quark is, the more mass it has. As a result, the up and down quarks are actually the lightest of the quarks. This down quark has a charge of -1/3.

name: Dracorex
description: A genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (around 66 million years ago). Its name, Dracorex hogwartsia, translates to \\"dragon king of Hogwarts,\\" inspired by its dragon-like appearance. Dracorex had a flat skull adorned with spiky horns and knobs. Though debated whether it represents a juvenile form of another species, such as Pachycephalosaurus, it was herbivorous or omnivorous and relatively small, around 10–12 feet (3–4 meters) long.

name: Duckweed
description: These are water lentils that float in ponds and other bodies of water. They don't have stems or roots. On the other hand, they do create flowers, which are the smallest of any plant on Earth.

name: Dust mite
description: Dust mites like to eat dust, which you probably know is just dead skin cells. Many people are allergic to dust mites. Some people are allergic to their poop. Either way, almost all people want to get rid of this pesky pest.

name: Dysnomia
description: Dysnomia is Eris's only moon and was discovered on September 10, 2005, which was a Saturday. Also, did you know that "Dysnomia" also refers to a law conflict?

name: E. coli
description: E. coli are usually harmless and live in your intestines, making wonderful vitamin K2. They are in almost everyone's intestines. In fact, they colonize babies' intestines just two days after they are born! Now that's pretty fast!

name: Eagle Nebula
description: The Eagle Nebula is a huge stellar nursery. Can you see the Pillars of Creation within it? Also, note that this image is based off of a false-color photograph. In true color, it's a bit more pinkish (as most large nebulae are).

name: Earth
description: The Earth is our only home. While living on it, we may consider it huge, but it is still important to take care of it. When it is gone, there will be nowhere else left to go.

name: Eiffel Tower
description: For 41 years, the Eiffel Tower was the tallest man-made structure in the world. 200 million people have been to the Eiffel Tower, which is almost the population of the U.S.A.!

name: Electron (Classical)
description: Electrons are so small that their size can't accurately be measured. The size of an electron varies greatly depending on how it's measured, whether it's based on the quantum model or, in this case, the classical model.

name: Elephant
description: The elephant is the largest land animal. Only three species of elephants remain. They can weigh up to 8 tons, which is about 10 times the weight any human has ever achieved!

name: Enif
description: Enif is an orange supergiant. Its name means "nose" in Arabic, because in the constellation Pegasus, it is in the position of the muzzle. Coincidentally, the word "Enif" backwards is "Fine".

name: Eris
description: Eris is the most massive dwarf planet. Although it's a little bit smaller, it is even heavier than Pluto, which is also a dwarf planet. Eris is also very far from the Sun. Eris has a moon, and that's Dysnomia. Where's Dysnomia? It's directly beneath the period at the end of this last sentence.

name: Eskimo Nebula
description: The Eskimo Nebula was discovered in 1787 and is the result of a Sun-like star dying and expanding into space. It supposedly looks like a head in the hood of a parka. Another name is the Clownhead Nebula.

name: Europa
description: Europa is the puniest of the Galilean moons. It is covered with ice. There are mysterious streaks that cover the moon's surface. Scientists think a water ocean might exist beneath the layer of ice, possibly even holding life.

name: FM Radio Wavelength
description: F and M are the sixth and thirteenth letters of the alphabet. Six is a perfect number, while thirteen is not. We all have our differences, and we have to learn to accept them. That's the only way we'll live in harmony.

name: Football Field
description: American football fields are 120 yards (109.7 m) long, if end zones are included. Anyway, football fields are often used for measuring large objects. For example, "The Titanic is roughly three football fields long!" Football fields are also used for playing football.

name: Gacrux
description: Gacrux can be found in the constellation Crux. Crux, which means "cross" in Latin, is known as the Southern Cross. We think Gacrux might be a binary star, but we haven't been able to spot its companion yet. The main star is a red giant.

name: Gamma Ray Wavelength
description: Gamma rays have a very high frequency. They can come from radioactive decay. Oh, and by the way, it's not actually light blue as it appears to the left.

name: Ganymede
description: Ganymede is the largest moon in the Solar System. It orbits Jupiter and was discovered by Galileo, which makes it a Galilean moon. Also, Ganymede is not to be confused with Ganymed, which is an asteroid whose name doesn't have the final "e"!

name: Gateway Arch
description: The Gateway Arch is the tallest structure in St. Louis. There is an observation area at the very top, and from it, you can see the Mississippi River. You should go visit it sometime in the near future!

name: Giant Earthworm
description: The giant earthworm can grow up to three meters long. When baby giant earthworms hatch from their cocoons, they are only twenty centimeters long. They take five years to reach their full length. They burrow underground and make gurgling noises.

name: Gigaparsec
description: A gigaparsec is one billion parsecs. It is one of the largest measures of distance. There's really no need to have any measure larger, because the radius of the observable universe is only 14 gigaparsecs.

name: Giraffe
description: The giraffe is the tallest land mammal at a mind-boggling 6 meters! Its neck alone is almost half of its entire height. Adult giraffes are so tall that the only way for predators to kill them is to push them onto their side!

name: Glass Marble
description: Glass marbles are round, but they're actually quite rough if you look at them under a microscope. This marble is an example of a toothpaste marble. Other marbles can be made of wood, plastic, metal, or even dirt.

name: Gliese 229A
description: Gliese 229A is a red dwarf star. It is a bit smaller than the Sun, but much less massive. The red dwarf star is also written as GJ 229 or GI 229. It is part of a binary star system, the second star being Gliese 229B.

name: Gliese 229B
description: Gliese 229B is a brown dwarf star. It orbits a larger dwarf star, which is Gliese 229A. Gliese 229B would be classified as a planet, but it is much denser than Jupiter, making its mass too large to be considered a planet.

name: Glucose Molecule
description: Glucose is a simple sugar. Plants make glucose through photosynthesis and turn it into complex sugars. When we (as humans) eat plants or animals that have eaten plants, we eat complex sugars, which we break down into glucose again, which we use for energy.

name: Gomez's Hamburger
description: Gomez's Hamburger is 900 light-years away! The buns are made of reflective dust, and the patty is made of dark dust. That does not sound very appetizing.

name: Grain of Rice
description: A lot of people on our beautiful world eat rice. If you eat one bowl of rice per day, you will eat around 300 million grains of rice in your lifetime. That is also approximately the population of the United States!

name: Grain of Salt
description: Salt is a compound made of a reactive metal and a toxic gas. Everyone knows that. Also, do not eat too much salt, or else you could die. Eating one thousandth of your body weight all at once will usually do the trick.

name: Grain of Sand
description: Of course, not all grains of sand are the same size. They vary a lot. Arenophiles are people who collect sand samples. Also, did you know that Papakolea Beach has green sand? This is because of crystals in the sand. Whatever.

name: Grand Canyon
description: The Grand Canyon is one of the largest canyons on Earth. It was carved by the Colorado River. All that carving took around 17 million years! The layers of rock exposed were even older, some as old as 1.8 billion years old!

name: Great Barrier Reef
description: The Great Barrier Reef, which is off the northeastern coast of Australia, is the biggest single structure made by living organisms! It is home to several thousand species of coral, mollusks, whales, turtles, dolphins, birds, and much more!

name: Great Nebula in Carina
description: This is also called the Eta Carinae Nebula, which is not to be confused with Eta Carinae's Homunculus Nebula (which is probably too small to see right now. It's 0.002 light-years across).

name: Great Pyramid of Giza
description: This picture is actually of the Giza Necropolis. The Great Pyramid of Giza is the pyramid on the left. It is the oldest one, built by the Egyptians in 2560 BC. It was the tallest man-made structure for almost 4,000 years.

name: Great Wall of China
description: The Great Wall of China is 2,900 kilometers across. However, it twists and turns, so if you straightened it out into a line, it would be over 8,800 kilometers long. The Great Wall of China is not visible from space. It's less than 10 meters wide! Most houses are wider than that!

name: Half Dome
description: Half Dome is a large dome that is in Yosemite National Park. It is made up of granite. The steep side of the dome is almost vertical. Don't fall off that side unless you want to. Some people like to climb it, even though it is very challenging!

name: Halley's Comet
description: The actual size of Halley's Comet is quite small. The tail that it forms can be millions of kilometers in length! Halley's comet loses 10% of its mass for every thousand orbits.

name: Helium Atom
description: Hydrogen atoms are larger than helium atoms because more protons in an atom's nucleus pull the electrons in closer. However, additional shells increase the size of the atoms the most. Cesium, the largest atom, has the most shells with the fewest electrons. (Francium has not been measured.)

name: Helium Nucleus
description: The helium nucleus is thousands of times smaller than the atom, like a marble in a football field. The only reason matter feels solid is because atoms repel. If atoms didn't repel, everything would fall through each other!

name: Helix Nebula
description: The Helix Nebula is one of the closest bright planetary nebulae to Earth. It is 650 light-years away from Earth! That's only six quadrillion (6,000,000,000,000,000) kilometers! (That is close, astronomically speaking.)

name: Hepatitis B Virus
description: Hepatitis B infects a lot of humans. In fact, almost one third of the world population has been infected at some point. This virus can cause vomiting, liver inflammation, and possibly death, but you most likely won't even notice if you have it at all!

name: High-Energy Neutrino
description: Neutrinos of higher energy are larger. For more about neutrinos, go to Neutrino, which shows the average size. It's a whopping 15,000 times smaller!

name: HIV
description: HIV, which stands for Human Immunodeficiency Virus, infects 0.5% of the world's population and causes AIDS, which stands for Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome. About 5 million new infections happen every year.

name: Homunculus Nebula
description: At the center of the Homunculus Nebula lies a star. The star, which is Eta Carinae, had a magnitude of -0.8 in 1841 (second brightest star in the sky). Now, it is a very dim sixth-magnitude star.

name: Hoover Dam
description: The Hoover Dam, which was built on the Colorado River, holds in the largest reservoir in the United States, Lake Mead. Not only does the Hoover Dam provide water, but its turbines also generate electricity.

name: Horsehead Nebula
description: The Horsehead Nebula is one of the most famous nebulae because it looks like a horse's head. It's a dark nebula in front of glowing pink gas. It was first discovered in 1888. Do you like all those 8s? I do.

name: Hourglass Nebula
description: The Hourglass Nebula is actually shaped like an hourglass. As you can see, there are two lobes. The upper lobe is tilted toward us, and the bottom one is tilted away.

name: Human
description: I'm going to assume you are a human. I am a human too! Did you know that there are almost 8 billion of us? That's a lot. In fact, if you met each person for one second, it would take you about 200 years to meet all of them! Better get started now!

name: Hummingbird
description: Hummingbirds flap their wings 12-80 times per second, which is about the same frequency as the lowest notes on the piano. They can also fly backwards and hover in mid-air. Amazing skills!

name: Hydra
description: Hydra is one of Pluto's moons. It's named after the seven-headed serpent from Greek mythology. Of course, Hydra will grow two heads if you cut one off. If you want Hydra to have n heads, all you have to do is cut off n-7 heads! However, this will not work if you want less than seven.

name: Hydrogen Atom
description: Hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe. It's usually just a proton and an electron! A hydrogen atom is so small you can't even see it with your naked eye, even if you try your hardest.

name: IC 1101
description: IC 1101 is the largest known galaxy. It contains about 100 trillion stars. Our galaxy, the Milky Way, has only 250 billion, so IC 1101 has 400 times more stars than the Milky Way.

name: Inch Ruler
description: For all of you people not accustomed to the metric system, here's an inch ruler, which is approximately one foot long. Unfortunately, almost everything else is in the metric system.

name: Infrared Wavelength
description: Things that are extremely rare are "inf-rare". (infinitely rare). Inf-rare is also a verb, meaning "to make inf-rare". (e.g., "John inf-rared the precious food by eating the last of it.") Infrared light used to be quite common, but one day, a scientist somehow "infrared" it.

name: Io
description: Io has a lot of volcanoes - the most in the Solar System! Io's geysers emit frozen sulfur dioxide up to 500 kilometers into space! The landscape of Io is constantly changing. In one year, it will look very different!

name: ISS
description: The International Space Station is pretty cool. It floats around in low Earth orbit, and you can even see it from Earth! The ISS has had humans within it continuously since November 2000, which is a record for a spacecraft. (They weren't all the same humans.)

name: Italy
description: Italy was the home to Leonardo Da Vinci, who was an inventor, mathematician, engineer, painter, sculptor, scientist, musician, and much more. Many other painters, including Raphael and Michelangelo, were also from Italy.

name: Japanese Spider Crab
description: The Japanese Spider Crab is the largest arthropod. However, not all Japanese Spider Crabs get to be this size. Some are only one meter across. Unfortunately, fishermen have killed many of them. That's a lot of crab meat!

name: Jupiter
description: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system. However, it is not the largest known planet. That's TrES-4b. Jupiter is a gas giant, so it's just a ball of gas. You can't land anywhere on it. Also, Jupiter has 80 moons, including the four Gallilean moons.

name: Kapteyn's Star
description: Kapteyn's Star is only thirteen light-years away. Even so, its magnitude is +9, so you need binoculars to see it. Around 10,000 years ago, Kapteyn's Star was only seven light-years away!

name: Kuiper Belt
description: The Kuiper Belt is a region of the Solar System outside the orbit of Neptune where small bodies orbit. Many dwarf planets exist here. It's like a larger asteroid belt.

name: KY Cygni
description: KY Cygni is 200,000 times more luminous than the Sun, and the Sun is pretty luminous, isn't it? The Sun is so luminous you aren't supposed to look at it! However, it's okay to look at KY Cyngi because it is 300 million times farther away.

name: La Superba
description: La Superba is one of the reddest stars in the night sky. It is so red because of all the carbon-13 it creates when fusing helium. (Normal carbon is carbon-12.) La Superba is only 2700° C, making it one of the coldest stars.

name: Lagoon Nebula
description: The Lagoon Nebula is a nebula that was named the Lagoon Nebula in 1747, the year it was discovered. It is 4,000 light-years away from us and includes tornadoes that emit UV light.

name: Large Hadron Collider
description: The Large Hadron Collider is the world's largest and highest-energy particle accelerator. It smashes protons into protons, and studies some of the tiniest things in the universe: quarks and leptons, and even the Higgs boson.

name: Large Magellanic Cloud
description: The Large Magellanic Cloud is only called large because it is larger than the galaxy nearest to it, the Small Magellanic Cloud. If the Small Magellanic Cloud were larger, the Large Magellanic Cloud would be called the Smaller Magellanic Cloud! We think the two galaxies used to be one, and were split apart by the Milky Way.

name: Largest Bacteria
description: The largest bacteria, which is Thiomargarita magnifica, is almost one centimeter in length. It was discovered in the swamps of Guadeloupe. Can you imagine being in the water and seeing these things? They're very visible!

name: Largest Hailstone
description: The largest hailstone was found in South Dakota. However, most hailstones don't even come close to this size. The average diameter is a measly centimeter. Oh, I would also like to point out that not all hailstones are round.

name: Largest Virus
description: The largest virus, known as Pithovirus, was discovered 30 meters undergound in the Siberian Permafrost. It's thought to have been there for 300,000 years; and still able to infect living things! Thankfully, it seems to only be interested in amoebae.

name: LCD Pixel
description: Although the size of pixels differs greatly, 300 micrometers is about the average. Pixels contain three subpixels that are red, green, and blue. Pixels are pretty cool, but voxels are even better. They are three-dimensional pixels.

name: Lengths shorter than this are not confirmed.
description: All the objects that are smaller than this are unmeasured. The sizes that they appear are only estimates. Some things, like quantum foam, are just hypothesized. They aren't fact.

name: Leo II Dwarf Galaxy
description: The Leo II Dwarf Galaxy is a satellite of the Milky Way Galaxy. It is one of the largest of around a hundred known satellites of the Milky Way. Like most dwarf galaxies, the stars in Leo II are metal-poor. They're also relatively old.

name: Light-Day
description: Even in just twenty-four hours, light manages to zoom across the distance of 26 billion kilometers. That is distance is further than any human or space probe has traveled.

name: Light-Year
description: In one year, light travels about 9.46 trillion kilometers. This distance is known as a light-year. The light-year is commonly used for measuring astronomical distances.

name: Luyten's Star
description: Luyten's Star is a red dwarf star 12 light-years away from us. It is so close to the star Procyon that Procyon appears 10 times brighter to Luyten's Star than Sirius appears to us.

name: Maip
description: A genus of large theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (approximately 70 million years ago) discovered in modern-day Patagonia. Named Maip macrothorax, it was a member of the megaraptorid family and measured around 33 feet (10 meters) long, and 11.4 feet (3.5 meters) wide. Maip was a fast predator, with large claws on its hands, slender legs, and a probable covering of protofeathers.

name: Maraapunisaurus Fragillimus
description: Maraapunisaurus fragillimus was the largest dinosaur ever! It weighed over 100 tons! It's so big you could build your house on top of it if you wanted to!

name: Marathon
description: A marathon is 42.2 kilometers, or 26.2 miles. About one thousandth of Americans have run a marathon. According to legend, after a Greek victory in the Battle of Marathon, Pheidippides was sent to Athens to tell the news. The distance was a marathon!

name: Mars
description: Mars is known as the Red Planet. It is red because it is rusty. Many people believe water and life once existed on Mars. However, even if that is true, the life wouldn't be like the little green men of sci-fi stories.

name: Matchstick
description: The matchstick is a very useful little tool. When struck against something, it can light on fire. This makes it very dangerous! That's why it's good to never play with matches. Got that?

name: Mercury
description: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun. It has no atmosphere. During the day, temperatures reach 430 °C, but at night, the temperatures fall to -180 °C. One Mercurian year is 88 Earth days.

name: Messier 54
description: Messier 54, or M54, or NGC 6715, is another globular cluster. It is in the Sagittarius Dwarf Galaxy, which would mean it's the first globular cluster outside of the Milky Way to be discovered.

name: Microwave Wavelength
description: Actually, the range of microwaves is pretty large. They range from one millimeter to one meter. So you see, this electromagnetic wave, with a wavelength of one centimeter, comfortably fits into this range and thus is a microwave!

name: Milky Way
description: The Milky Way is the galaxy we live in. You can't see the whole thing at once, of course, but on a dark night you might be able to see a streak that spans the whole sky! It looks milky, so it's the Milky Way.

name: Mimivirus
description: This little thingy was discovered in 1992 within an amoeba, which is only 500 times larger. Mimivirus used to be the largest virus known, but now that is the Pithovirus.

name: Minecraft World
description: Stretching from the coordinates +32,000,000 to -32,000,000, the Minecraft world spans a total of 64,000 kilometers. This world has approximately 130 quadrillion blocks in it! That sure is a lot!

name: Mist droplet
description: Mist can reduce visibility a bit. It can also reflect light, so that you can see rays of light shining through it. That's pretty cool. If you want to find mist, go to the coast or the mountains. Mist is more common there.

name: Mitochondrion
description: These organelles can turn the chemical energy from glucose into ATP, which can easily be used for energy. About 120,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000 ATP molecules are created every day in your body by mitochondria.

name: Moon
description: The Moon, also known as "Luna” is the brightest object in the night sky. It orbits Earth every 27 days, 7 hours and 43 minutes. I like to imagine seeing several moons scattered across the night sky. That would be amazing!

name: Mount Everest
description: Mount Everest is the highest mountain on Earth at 8,848 meters. It is constantly growing, like children do. Not many people can climb it without using an oxygen tank.

name: Mu Cephei
description: Mu Cephei is very red. It is sometimes referred to as "Herschel's Garnet Star" because it is garnet red. Another alternative name is Erakis, if you don't like the first two names.

name: Neptune
description: Currently, Neptune is the farthest planet in the Solar System from the Sun. It takes 164.8 Earth years for it to orbit the Sun. You'll never see Neptune complete a single orbit in your whole life, unless life expectancies increase.

name: Neutrino
description: Neutrinos pass through ordinary matter like you and me all the time! In fact, they're doing it right now! Neutrinos have no charge, so they are only affected by gravity and the weak force. However, they are so small that they are barely affected.

name: Neutron
description: Neutrons are found within an atom's nucleus. They are thousands of times smaller than the atom itself. They have two down quarks and one up quark. Therefore, the neutron's charge is -1/3-1/3+2/3 = 0.

name: Neutron Star
description: Neutron stars are made of tightly packed neutrons. As a result, they are extremely dense. One milliliter of neutron star material weighs 1 billion kilograms!

name: NGC 1232
description: NGC 1232 is a pretty big galaxy. It's bigger than ours, but it's not the biggest. Of course, IC 1101 is not the biggest galaxy, it's only the biggest galaxy we know of. There are probably many more!

name: NGC 3310
description: NGC 3310 is another one of those galaxies that have collided with other galaxies, altering their shape. It is a spiral galaxy that has very beautiful arms and is around 35 million light-years away.

name: NGC 4889
description: This very large galaxy, which is also known as Caldwell 35, is the brightest galaxy in the Coma cluster. It is going away from us at 6,500 kilometers per second. At that speed, it could circumnavigate the Earth in less than seven seconds!

name: Nix
description: Nix is the smallest known moon of Pluto. Its name is the second shortest name of a moon, the shortest being Io. The name comes from the primordial Greek goddess Nyx, who was the night.

name: North America Nebula
description: The apparent size of the North America Nebula is very large. It appears almost four times as large as a full moon. It's just very dim... so... you know...you need longer exposure times to see it.

name: Oak Tree
description: The oak tree is a tree. They can live a very long time, longer than even a human can! Oak trees produce thousands of acorns a year. Acorns are good. Almost as good as ice cubes.

name: Observable Universe
description: The radius, which is half of the diameter, is about 46 billion light-years. You might expect to only see 13.7 billion light-years away because light has had only 13.7 billion years to travel, but due to the expansion of space, the objects that used to be 13.7 billion light-years away are now 46 billion light-years away, and we can see these.

name: Omega Centauri
description: Omega Centauri is a globular cluster. It is the brightest globular cluster. The center is very crowded. Stars there are 0.1 light-years apart, which is 2% of the distance from our Sun to the nearest star. (That is really close!)

name: Oort Cloud
description: The Oort Cloud is the outermost region of the solar system. Water ice, ammonia ice, methane ice and comets loosely fill the region. The boundary of the Oort Cloud is also the boundary of the Sun's gravitational pull.

name: Orion Nebula
description: The Orion Nebula is one of the most visible nebulae ever! As the name suggests, it lies in the constellation Orion. Did you know that bullets of gas exist in this nebula?

name: Ovum (Human Egg)
description: An ovum is the largest cell in the human body. Animals that develop within eggs within their mother are ovoviviparous, which humans are not. Ovoviviparous is the only English word starting with "vowel-v-vowel-v-vowel-v-vowel".

name: Palm Jebel Ali
description: Palm Jebel Ali is the largest man-made island (not including islands like Flevopolder, where a region of the Netherlands was drained so there could be more land). Palm Jebel Ali is not complete. It is part of the Palm Islands and is planned to house 250,000 people!

name: Paramecium
description: Paramecia vary quite a lot in size, between 50 and 330 micrometers. These little guys are protists and like to swim in water. They can move two millimeters per second!

name: Parsec
description: Earth's movement around the Sun causes nearby stars to appear to move, called parallax. If a nearby star is perpendicular to the solar system and is exactly one parsec away, its parallax will be exactly one arcsecond (1/3600°). Parallax + Arcsec = Parsec

name: Patagotitan
description: A colossal sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (about 100 million years ago), closely related to Argentinosaurus. Found in Patagonia, it is estimated to have reached lengths of up to 122 feet (37 meters) and weighed 55–77 tons. It was a slow-moving herbivore, relying on its size for protection against predators.

name: Pencil Lead
description: People use pencil lead a lot. Oh, by the way, this is mechanical pencil lead. On another note, the largest pencil contained 2,000 kilograms of graphite. That's about three times heavier than a human! And by human I mean the heaviest human ever.

name: Phobos
description: Phobos is the larger of the two Martian moons. Still, it is quite small! Did you know that if you were standing on Phobos, its gravity would be so weak that you could throw a baseball and it wouldn't come back down?

name: Phospholipid
description: Phospholipids are beautifully amazing little molecules, and you have millions of millions of millions of them in your body. Phospholipids are actually a special type of lipid that make up cell membranes.

name: Phospholipid Bilayer
description: Cell membranes are made of phospholipid bilayers. Phospholipid bilayers can form naturally because the heads attract to water and the tails repel from water. As a result, tails clump together, forming the bilayer.

name: Pillars of Creation
description: The Pillars of Creation lie within the Eagle Nebula. The Eagle Nebula is 7,000 light-years away from us. Evidence shows that the Pillars of Creation were destroyed by a supernova about 6,000 years ago, so we only have another millennium to see it.

name: Pinwheel Galaxy
description: When this galaxy was first discovered, it was believed to be a nebula. It's not; it's a galaxy. It's also called Messier 101, because it was the 101st Messier object to be cataloged.

name: Pistol Star
description: The Pistol Star is one of the most luminous stars known. In twenty seconds, it radiates as much energy as the Sun does in a year. Interstellar dust prevents us from seeing it. It's called the Pistol Star not because of its luminosity, but because it's in the Pistol Nebula.

name: Planck Length
description: The planck length (lp) is a unit of length. There is also a Planck time, which is the amount of time it takes light to travel one Planck length in a vacuum. (Super short!)

name: Pluto
description: Pluto is no longer a planet. Why do people feel sympathy for it? It has no feelings. Even if it did, why would it care about what people way over on Earth thought? On another note, Pluto's official name is now "134340 Pluto” because it's a dwarf planet.

name: Polaris
description: Polaris is known as the North Star. It is very near the north pole, so if you want to know which way is north, just try to find Polaris. Locate the two stars on the far side of the Big Dipper, and go upward.

name: Pollux
description: In Greek mythology, Pollux's twin is Castor. They are also stars in the constellation Gemini, meaning "twins". However, in reality, Pollux is a single star, while Castor is three binary stars, for a total of six stars. Castor is a set of sextuplets!

name: Porcine circovirus
description: The Porcine circovirus is one of the smallest viruses. It only requires three proteins to replicate. If you consider viruses to be alive, then the Porcine circovirus is the smallest living thing. However, scientists disagree whether viruses are alive or not.

name: Procyon
description: Did you know that over the next 10 million years, Procyon will grow to 100 times its current diameter and become red? This is because Procyon's hydrogen is now all helium. The Sun will do the same thing in a couple billion years.

name: Proton
description: Protons are found within an atom's nucleus. They are thousands of times smaller than the atom itself. They have two up quarks and one down quark. Therefore, the proton's charge is +2/3+2/3-1/3 = +1.

name: Proxima Centauri
description: Proxima Centauri is known as being the closest star to Earth other than the Sun. Its name comes from the word "proximity". Proxima Centauri is a small red star. Unfortunately, it is so dim, you need a telescope to see it.

name: Psittacosaurus
description: A small, parrot-beaked ceratopsian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (approximately 125–100 million years ago). It measured about 6.5 feet (2 meters) in length and weighed around 44 pounds (20 kilograms). Known for its distinctive short beak, similar to a parrot's, Psittacosaurus was a herbivore, feeding on tough vegetation. Some species had long filament-like structures on their tails, which might have been used for display.

name: Pumpkin
description: Pumpkin is the name of this adorable little cat that belongs to the artist who drew these dinosaurs! This may not be a dinosaur... but this gives a great sense of scale when comparing the gigantic dinosaurs of the past!

name: Quail Egg
description: Many people think that quail eggs are better than chicken eggs. The quail egg is smaller and more rare than the chicken egg, but that doesn't stop some people from loving them!

name: Quantum Foam
description: According to the spacetime theory, quantum foam, which is also known as spacetime foam, is the foundation of the fabric of the universe. It is impossible to directly observe or measure this because it is so small.

name: Quaoar
description: Some scientists used to think Quaoar had a dense core because a pluto-sized object hit it and stripped away its outer layers, but now we know Quaoar is much lighter than we originally assumed. Even so, it's almost as heavy and big as Pluto's moon Charon,

name: R Doradus
description: R Doradus is relatively large and is only 200 light-years from Earth, so its apparent size is the second largest in the sky! It is 0.05 arcseconds across. The Sun is the largest, at 1,920 arcseconds - 40,000 times larger.

name: Rafflesia
description: The Rafflesia is the largest single flower in the world. It can weigh up to 10 kilograms, which is actually pretty heavy. The flower smells like rotting flesh to attract flies, which will pollinate it.

name: Range of the Weak Force
description: The weak force is one of the four fundamental forces of nature, and is the weaker of the two nuclear forces. As distance increases, its strength decreases. At just 10 attometers, the weak force is so weak it is unmeasurable.

name: Red Blood Cell
description: Around one quarter of all human cells are red blood cells. They circulate through one cycle every 20 seconds, and only live around four months. The dent allows for more surface area, which means it's easier to transport oxygen. Oh, and they have no nucleus.

name: Red Light Wavelength
description: Red is the first color of the rainbow, and the longest wavelength of light we humans can see. It stimulates appetite. That's why so many food companies have red in their logo. They want you to eat! In short, they're trying to fool your brain.

name: Redwood Tree
description: The redwood tree is the tallest tree species in the world. Its height, combined with that of an ant's, is greater than any human's! However, the universe is still 10^25 times larger.

name: Regulus
description: Regulus spins so quickly it bulges, just like Altair and Vega. If Regulus were to spin 16% faster, it would rip apart! Regulus is also the brightest star in the constellation Leo. There's also Regulus B & C.

name: Rhamphornychus
description: A genus of long-tailed pterosaurs from the Late Jurassic (about 150 million years ago). With a wingspan of approximately 3–6 feet (1–2 meters), Rhamphorhynchus had a lightweight body, long jaws with sharp teeth, and a distinctive diamond-shaped vane at the end of its tail. It likely fed on fish, using its long teeth to snatch prey from the water while in flight.

name: Rhode Island
description: Rhode Island is the smallest state in the U. S. A. However, it could still fit the world's population on it. Did you know that Rhode Island has the longest official state name? (The State of Rhode Island and Providence Plantations)

name: Rigel
description: Rigel is a blazing blue star, and the brightest star in the constellation of Orion. It lies near the equator, so it is visible from almost anywhere on Earth. Also, don't call it "wriggle" because it's pronounced like "rye-jel".

name: Ring Nebula
description: The Ring Nebula looks a lot like a ring. The old belief was that all planetary nebulae were ring-shaped like this one, but that is now known to not be true. There are other shapes planetary nebulae can be.

name: Rosette Nebula
description: The Rosette Nebula is very large, but very faint. It looks like a fiery rose and a rosy fire. It's approximately 5,000 light-years away from us and has a mass of 10,000 Suns. (3 billion Earths)

name: Rotten Egg Nebula
description: The Rotten Egg Nebula has sulfur in it. That's probably why this nebula is called the Rotten Egg Nebula. I think it looks a bit like a raw egg, where the blue is the cracked shell and the yellow is the yolk spilling out.

name: Russell's Teapot
description: Bertrand Russell said that there was a teapot orbiting the Sun between Earth and Mars. No one could disprove him because such a teapot would be too tiny to see so far away. Even though the teapot's existence could not be disproven, it seems illogical to believe it exists.

name: Rwanda
description: Rwanda is a country in eastern-central Africa and is known as "The Land of a Thousand Hills". Did you know that Rwanda as a country ranks 144th in land area? That means there are only 143 countries larger than it.

name: S Doradus
description: S Doradus is a hypergiant, as well as one of the brightest stars in the Large Magellanic Cloud. For more about the Large Magellanic Cloud, zoom out 8.5 orders of magnitude.

name: Sagittarius Dwarf Galaxy
description: The Sagittarius Dwarf Spheroidal Galaxy is one of the Milky Way's satellite galaxies. It is only 65,000 light-years distant! It is an elliptical galaxy, and a dwarf one as well. It was not discovered until 1994, because it's on the opposite side of the Milky Way from us.

name: Saguaro Cactus
description: Not all Saguaro cacti grow to be 14 meters tall, unfortunately. Some may only be a measly five meters tall. Some are armless - those are called spears. It takes saguaro cacti 75 years to fully grow an arm, while it takes humans less than 20.

name: Saturn
description: Saturn has the widest, most visible rings of the Solar System. Galileo, who was the first person to see these rings, first thought they were "ears". Can you imagine a Saturn without rings? It would be just a yellow ball.

name: Saturn V
description: Saturn V is the tallest and heaviest rocket ever! That's pretty cool. It was used from 1968-1972 and was the only vehicle used to launch humans far away from the Earth, to places such as the Moon.

name: Sedna
description: Sedna has a very elliptical orbit. It is so far away it takes 10,000 Earth years to complete one orbit. You probably realized that "Sedna" backwards is "Andes”, the world's longest mountain range (It's in South America). Both may seem other-worldly!

name: Shrew
description: Shrews are great little thingies. Their metabolism rate is so high they must eat their body weight every single day! Also, 10% of their body weight is their brain. That's the highest of any animal, including humans!

name: Silt Particle
description: According to the Udden-Wentworth scale, silt particles vary from the smallest of 1/256 mm to the largest of 1/16 mm. This silt particle to the right falls into that range quite well.

name: Sirius A
description: Sirius is the brightest star in the night sky. It is almost twice as bright as Canopus, the second brightest star. However, Sirius is not a large star. It only appears bright because it is close to us, at only eight light-years away.

name: Sirius B
description: Sirius B is a white dwarf. It's barely bigger than the Earth! It's usually pretty hard to see because it is hidden by Sirius A's blinding light. Sirius B is known as "The Pup".

name: Skin Cell
description: The outermost layer of skin, which is also the only visible layer of skin, is made up of dead cells. That's because dead cells are the strongest, and stronger skin provides more protection! These cells continuously fall off. In fact, dust is 95% dead skin cells!

name: Sleet
description: Sleet, which falls from the sky, is made of ice pellets. That ball to the left is an ice pellet. Sleet is similar to snow, but it's much denser. You also may have noticed that sleet is significantly smaller than hail.

name: Small Magellanic Cloud
description: The Small Magellanic Cloud is only called small because it is smaller than the galaxy nearest to it, the Large Magellanic Cloud. If the Large Magellanic Cloud were smaller, the Small Magellanic Cloud would be called the Large Magellanic Cloud!

name: Smallest Object Visible to the Naked Eye
description: Under optimal conditions, the naked eye can see objects as small as 0.1 millimeters. That means that anything smaller than this circle is impossible for you to see without a microscope. You're missing out quite a lot, you know!

name: Smallest Thing Visible to an Electron Microscope
description: Electron microscopes allow us to see very small things (like atoms) that would otherwise be invisible because they are smaller than the wavelength of visible light.

name: Smallest Thing Visible to an Optical Microscope
description: Optical microscopes cannot see objects smaller than 200 nanometers because the wavelengths of visible light will pass right through them.

name: Sombrero Galaxy
description: Do you think the Sombrero Galaxy looks like a sombrero? I'm not sure. Oh! Did you know that there is a supermassive black hole in the center of this galaxy? Most large galaxies have supermassive black holes, even our Milky Way!

name: Spica
description: Spica is the fifteenth brightest star in the night sky and lies in the constellation Virgo. It's actually a binary star system. The two stars are very close, and they orbit every four days.

name: Square Inch
description: If you aren't accustomed to the metric system, here's a square inch. Unfortunately for you, almost everything else is in the metric system. Also, 2.5 centimeters is the length of one side, not the area. (That would be in cm^2.)

name: Statue of Liberty
description: Although the Statue of Liberty is a symbol of America, it was actually built in France. It was based off of an ancient statue of the Greek Sun god Helios. The Statue of Liberty is pretty cool, even though the torch has been closed since 1916 because it is dangerous.

name: Stingray Nebula
description: The Stingray Nebula is a planetary nebula. It is relatively young, and is constantly growing. Whoever named this nebula must have thought it looked like a stingray. Do you?

name: Strange Quark
description: Strange quarks are very strange. They are 50 times as massive as up quarks, but are still smaller! Isn't that strange? Very much so! "Strange matter" is made up of up, down, and strange quarks!

name: String
description: According to string theory, strings are one-dimensional, but vibrate in all the other dimensions. This string, however, is not the same string that is used for flying kites. That string is between 10^32 and 10^33 times larger.

name: Sun
description: Also known as Sol, the Sun is what gives Earth the energy that makes it able to have life. The Sun is over 100 times larger in diameter than Earth! 20,000 times more solar energy hits the Earth than the human race uses.

name: Sunflower
description: Did you know that the "flower" of a sunflower is not a single flower, but actually a flower head made up of thousands of small flowers? These small flowers are called florets. The florets in the center become sunflower seeds, which are currently invisible at this scale.

name: Sunflower Seed
description: Sunflower seeds are exhilaratingly delicious. They are so yummy, they can be made into "sunflower seed bread"! Sunflower seeds are not real seeds. They are the fruits of the sunflower! They are only called "seeds" because they resemble other seeds.

name: Tadpole Galaxy
description: The tadpole galaxy has a trail of stars. They were probably formed when another galaxy collided into it. Just like tadpoles, the Tadpole Galaxy is expected to lose its tail as it gets older.

name: Tarantula Nebula
description: The Tarantula Nebula is the largest known nebula. It can be found in the Large Magellanic cloud. It's very bright, with an absolute magnitude is -11. It is the brightest nebula ever discovered.

name: Texas
description: Texas is the only state to have the same rank in both population and land area, which is second. If Puerto Rico were to be counted, Mississippi would rank in 32nd in both categories, but it's not, because Puerto Rico is not a state.

name: Thickness of Paper
description: Paper is very thin. It's so thin it can give you a paper cut. You can do a lot of things with paper, including writing on it, drawing on it, and even folding it into shapes like squares and triangles and dodecahedrons and others.

name: Thou
description: Although the word "thou" is most commonly known as being an old form of the word "you", a thou is also a unit of measurement. It is equal to one thousandth of an inch; in fact, that's where it gets its name. THOUsandth. Pretty cool.

name: Titan
description: Titan is Saturn's only large moon. It has a very thick atmosphere, which is mostly made up of nitrogen. Earth's atmosphere is also mostly nitrogen. Titan has seas of liquid methane. (It's around -180 °C.) There's even a continent called "Xanadu".

name: Titanic
description: The Titanic sank when it hit an iceberg. Although it was capable of holding enough lifeboats for 4,000 people, they decided they only needed enough lifeboats for 1,178 people. There were 2,228 people on the Titanic.

name: Top Quark
description: The top quark is the smallest quark, which means it is the most massive. It is almost 100,000 times as massive as the up quark, which is the lightest of the quarks. The top quark's mass is 173 billion electronvolts!

name: Total Human Height
description: If all the 7.8 billion humans stood on top of one another, and the bottom 7.7 billion humans didn't crumple under all the weight, the stack would be approximately 13 million kilometers tall.

name: Transistor Gate
description: Transistors in computers are continuing to get smaller. In 1971, they were 400 times larger at 10 micrometers. Now we are down to 5 nanometers, but in the future, they might be even smaller. How small will they get? Moore's Law predicts we can make new transistors half as big as before every 2 years.

name: TrES-4b
description: TrES-4b is one of the largest planets discovered. No telescope can actually see TrES-4b, but there are ways to prove its existence indirectly. TrES-4b's gravity causes the star it orbits to wobble slightly. Also, whenever TrES-4b goes in front of the star, the shine of the star dims a little.

name: Triangulum Galaxy
description: This galaxy is not shaped like a triangle; it's just called that because it is found in the constellation Triangulum. Did you realize that any three stars form a "triangle constellation"? You could have millions of triangle constellations!

name: Triton
description: Triton is Neptune's largest moon. It is also the coldest object we've visited in the Solar System, at -240 ° C. Triton is also a very windy place, with winds going at almost the speed of sound!

name: Twip
description: A twip is a twentieth of a typographical point. That means that 12-point font is approximately 240 twips high. Also, TUIP can stand for "The Universe in Perspective”, an old name for the Scale of the Universe. TUIP and twip are pronounced the same.

name: Tyrannosaurus
description: One of the most famous and fearsome dinosaurs, T. rex lived during the Late Cretaceous (around 68–66 million years ago). As an apex predator, it measured up to 40 feet (12 meters) in length, stood about 13–15 feet (4–5 meters) tall at the hips, and weighed between 9–14 tons. It had powerful jaws lined with teeth capable of crushing bone, a robust skull, and tiny but functional arms. Its diet included large prey like hadrosaurs and ceratopsians.

name: Tyrannosaurus Rex
description: This dinosaur is now extinct, but it used to be up to 7 meters long. Although the T-Rex was very large, it was not the largest! Zoom out a bit to see the largest dinosaur!

name: Ultraviolet Wavelength
description: Ultraviolet light can give you a tan. I used to think the sunlight and heat burnt your skin to make it darker, like how a barbecue grill turns all of the food black. Now I know that's not true.

name: Uluru
description: Uluru, which is also known as Ayers Rock, is a rock. At 348 meters tall, it is shorter than many buildings. However, it is three kilometers in length, making it longer than all buildings.

name: United States of America
description: Here is the U. S. A. Although it may seem to be larger than Pluto, Pluto has a back face, which means Pluto has more surface area!

name: Estimated Size of the Universe
description: The Universe is everything we know. Oh, also, the universe may be much larger than this, or could even be infinite! There's just no way to accurately measure the size. There may also be multiverses, too, but we'll never know for sure.

name: Up Quark
description: There are six flavors of quarks. They are up, down, strange, charm, top, and bottom. The smaller a quark is, the more mass it has. As a result, the up and down quarks are actually the lightest of the quarks. This up quark has a charge of +2/3.

name: Uranium Nucleus
description: Uranium is the heaviest natural element. It also has the largest nucleus of all the natural elements. Uranium-238, the most common isotope of uranium, has a half-life of 4.5 billion years, approximately the time the Earth has existed.

name: Uranus
description: A lot of people like to think "Uranus" sounds like "your anus". There are many other, and better, pronunciations. Uranus can also be pronounced like "Yer-uh-niss" or "Seh-venth-pla-net."

name: U.S. Penny
description: Did you know that in 1912, the penny was worth what the quarter is worth now? Also, if you melt a penny down into zinc and copper, it would be worth 2.5 cents. That is illegal, so don't do it.

name: V354 Cephei
description: V354 Cephei is a red supergiant, and it's huge. If placed in the Solar System, it would extend out to the asteroid belt. 354, in addition to being in this star's name, is also the year Augustine of Hippo was born.

name: Vatican City
description: Vatican City is the smallest country. It, although very small, is larger than you are. If you were to stretch your flesh over Vatican City so that it was spread evenly, the coating would be about 200 nanometers thick, which is less than even a single skin cell!

name: Vega
description: Vega is found in the Lyra constellation. It is also part of the Summer Triangle, composed of Vega, Altair, and Deneb. Vega spins so quickly it bulges. However, we see it pole-on, so it looks circular.

name: Venus
description: There was a time when Venus and Earth were thought to be sister planets. That isn't true at all! Venus's surface temperature is always above 450 °C, because its atmosphere, which is mostly carbon dioxide, traps the Sun's heat!

name: Violet Light Wavelength
description: Also known as mauve, purple, or even regalia, violet, which can be called lavender, periwinkle or iris, and sometimes even amethyst or eggplant, is also referred to as fuchsia, indigo or lilac, and on rare occasions, byzantium, thistle, wisteria, fandango and heliotrope.

name: Virgo A
description: Virgo A is the largest galaxy in the Virgo Cluster. It's a supergiant elliptical galaxy. Did you know that the black hole at its center is flinging out a jet of subatomic matter 5,000 light-years long? The supermassive black hole in its middle, M87* (prounounced em-eighty-seven-star), is the first black hole we ever imaged.

name: VV Cephei A
description: For a while VV Cephei A was the largest known star. Now that title belongs to Stephenson 2-18. That's because new stars are being discovered all the time!

name: VY Canis Majoris
description: VY Canis Majoris is one of the largest known stars. It is so large that if it were placed in the Solar System, it would extend beyond the orbit of Saturn! It would take a plane 800 years to circle it once.

name: Wandering Albatross
description: The Wandering Albatross is the bird with the largest wingspan! They fly almost all the time, only stopping to eat and make babies. Their population is slowly decreasing. Now it is only at 26,000, which isn't very much.

name: Washington Monument
description: The Washington Monument is the world's tallest obelisk, as well as the world's tallest stone structure. It was the world's tallest structure from 1884 to 1889. After that it was the Eiffel Tower, which is to the upper-right.

name: Water Molecule
description: I like to think that whenever I drink water, I drink Mickey Mouse heads. Do you, too? Oh, you don't? Well, you should.

name: West Virginia
description: West Virginia used to be part of Virginia. They split in 1863 because of the Civil War. Now, West Virginia is still a state! A lot of glass marbles are made in West Virginia.

name: Whirlpool Galaxy
description: The spiral arms in the Whirlpool Galaxy were believed to have been formed from a collision of a nearby galaxy. Wouldn't it be odd if you grew new arms whenever you collided into something?

name: White Blood Cell
description: These mass murderers are also known as leukocytes. There are many different types of this disease-fighting cell. The most common type, the neutrophil, has multiple nuclei. The largest type, the macrophage, has a diameter of a whopping 21 µm.

name: Width of Human Hair
description: Human hair is amazing. Straight hair is almost perfectly cylindrical. On the other hand, curly hair is flatter, which allows it to curl, like a ribbon. Did you know that you probably have 50,000 to 200,000 strands of hair on your head? You can count!

name: Width of Silk Fiber
description: Silk is valuable and can be woven into clothing, which usually turns out quite soft. Silk is made by silkworm larvae, which I think are pretty gross. But there's also synthetic silk, which is not made from disgusting creepy-crawlies.

name: WOH G64
description: I know... you're thinking, "WOH... that's a big star!" Well, I guess you're right, because it's one of the biggest known stars. WOH G64 is found in the Large Magellanic Cloud. There is a cloud surrounding the star that extends half of a light-year.

name: Wolf 359
description: Wolf 359 is a small red dwarf, dwarfed by normal stars. It is the sixth closest star to Earth, after the Sun, Proxima Centauri, Alpha Centauri A & B and Barnard's Star. Still, its magnitude is a dim +13.5.

name: X Chromosome
description: X chromosomes are absolutely wonderful. Most chromosomes look like X's even if they aren't sex chromosomes – those are autosomes. In humans, females have two X chromosomes while males have an X and a Y chromosome. Some people might have three, four, or even five chromosomes, though.

name: X-Ray Wavelength
description: Although X-rays do not have the highest frequency, it's still a bad idea to bask in them. Don't do it! Usually, you should have a lead shield. The thicker the lead is, the more protection. However, a few X-rays will always get through. Thicker lead just lowers this chance.

name: Y Chromosome
description: The Y chromosome, a sex chromosome, appears only in males. Did you know that the Y chromosome is continuing to shrink as it evolves, and might possibly disappear completely? I find that to be very fascinating! Some animals, like the Amami Spiny Rat, have already lost their Y chromosome.
